title: Paralysis After Trauma - Spinal Cord Injury
chief_complaint: 22-year-old male dove into shallow pool, hit head on bottom. Now unable to move arms or legs. Loss of sensation below shoulders. Priapism noted.

physical_exam.neuro: Flaccid paralysis all 4 limbs (quadriplegia), sensory level at C6, loss of rectal tone, priapism, diaphragmatic breathing only, hypotension with bradycardia (neurogenic shock)

hidden_data.ct_cervical_spine: Burst fracture C5 with retropulsion of bone fragments into spinal canal, cord compression at C5-C6
hidden_data.mri_cervical: Spinal cord contusion and edema at C5-C6 with intramedullary hemorrhage

correct_diagnosis: Complete Spinal Cord Injury at C5-C6 (ASIA A) with Neurogenic Shock
key_learning_points: Neurogenic shock = hypotension + bradycardia (loss of sympathetic tone)

patient_responses.accident: I dove into the shallow end of the pool
patient_responses.symptoms: I can't feel or move anything below my shoulders
patient_responses.breathing: I feel like I can't take a deep breath

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Complete SCI with neurogenic shock.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a spinal cord injury. The paralysis + sensory level + imaging confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: SCI management: Spine immobilization, MAP >85 (vasopressors if needed, dopamine or norepinephrine), early surgical decompression within 24h, DVT prophylaxis, stress ulcer prophylaxis, bowel/bladder program, rehabilitation. Steroids NOT routinely recommended per current guidelines.
